name: Kushinadahime
shortDescription: Kushinadahime is a princess of Izumo who is saved from the Yamata no Orochi by Susanoo-no-Mikoto, later becoming his wife.
description: Kushinadahime, also known as Kushiinadahime or Inadahime, is a significant figure in Japanese mythology, primarily known for her role in the legend of Susanoo-no-Mikoto's battle against the Yamata no Orochi, the eight-headed serpent. She is the last of eight daughters of the deities Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi, who were forced to sacrifice their daughters to the monstrous serpent. When Susanoo arrives in Izumo, he encounters the grieving parents. He learns of Kushinadahime's impending fate and devises a plan to slay the Yamata no Orochi. Susanoo transforms Kushinadahime into a comb to keep her safe during the battle. He then instructs Ashinazuchi and Tenazuchi to prepare eight vats of strong sake, which he uses to intoxicate the serpent. While the Orochi is incapacitated, Susanoo slays it, freeing the land from its terror. After the battle, Susanoo restores Kushinadahime to her original form, and they marry. They eventually settle in Suga, where Susanoo builds a palace and declares, 'My heart is at peace'. Kushinadahime is considered a symbol of purity, sacrifice, and rebirth. Her story highlights themes of divine intervention, heroism, and the triumph of good over evil. Her role is crucial to the narrative of Susanoo's redemption from a destructive deity to a heroic figure. She is also associated with rice fields, as her name contains the element 'Inada' which can refer to rice paddies.
